title: App lifecycle and system reclamation
description: iOS moves apps through active/background/suspended states and can terminate suspended apps to reclaim resources without warning.
body: Use when handling backgrounding or persistence. iOS transitions an app between foreground-active, background (briefly), and suspended; a suspended app does no work and can be terminated at any time to free memory, often with no callback. Background execution is a limited, API-gated privilege, not a default. Do not assume your process keeps running or gets a clean shutdown. Actions: persist state when entering background, use the proper background API for needed work, and restore on relaunch. Validation signals include correct restoration after a system kill. Risk boundary: assuming continuous background execution loses in-flight work when the system reclaims the app, and unsaved state at suspension is simply gone after termination — design for abrupt suspension.
